title: calophyllum lanigerum var. lanigerum
intro: Calophyllum lanigerum var. lanigerum, also known as the woolly camphor tree, is a tropical plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its thick, grey bark and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of camphor and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum lanigerum var. lanigerum have been associated with various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, and has been used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory infections.
